info.description: Convoy is a fast and secure webhooks proxy. This document contains datastore.s API specification.
info.title: Convoy API Reference

post.description: This endpoint replays multiple events at once.
This endpoint creates a event that is broadcast to every endpoint whose subscription matches the given event type.
This endpoint does not require creating endpoint and subscriptions ahead of time. Instead, you supply the endpoint and the payload, and Convoy delivers the events
This endpoint uses the owner_id to fan out an event to multiple endpoints.

get.description: This endpoint retrieves an event

put.description: This endpoint replays an event afresh assuming it is a new event.

can_manage_endpoint.description: Specify whether endpoint management can be done through the Portal Link UI

endpoints.description:
Deprecated
IDs of endpoints in this portal link

owner_id.description: OwnerID, the portal link will inherit all the endpoints with this owner ID

data.description:
Data is an arbitrary JSON value that gets sent as the body of the
webhook to the endpoints
Data to be sent to endpoint.

num_trials.description:
NumTrials: number of times we have tried to deliver this Event to
an application

client_cert.description: ClientCert is the client certificate PEM string

client_key.description: ClientKey is the client private key PEM string

expiry_time_unit.description: Expiry time unit (seconds, milliseconds, minutes, hours)

field_mapping.description: Field mapping for flexible token response parsing

dp.description: RSA first factor CRT exponent (RSA private key only)

dq.description: RSA second factor CRT exponent (RSA private key only)

p.description: RSA first prime factor (RSA private key only)

q.description: RSA second prime factor (RSA private key only)

qi.description: RSA first CRT coefficient (RSA private key only)

can_manage_endpoint.description: Specify whether endpoint management can be done through the Portal Link UI

endpoints.description:
Deprecated
IDs of endpoints in this portal link

owner_id.description: OwnerID, the portal link will inherit all the endpoints with this owner ID

custom_headers.description: Specifies custom headers you want convoy to add when the event is dispatched to your endpoint

data.description:
Data is an arbitrary JSON value that gets sent as the body of the
webhook to the endpoints

event_type.description: Event Type is used for filtering and debugging e.g invoice.paid

idempotency_key.description: Specify a key for event deduplication

advanced_signatures.description:
Convoy supports two [signature formats](https://getconvoy.io/docs/product-manual/signatures)
-- simple or advanced. If left unspecified, we default to false.

appID.description: Deprecated but necessary for backward compatibility

authentication.description:
This is used to define any custom authentication required by the endpoint. This
shouldn't be needed often because webhook endpoints usually should be exposed to
the internet.

content_type.description: Content type for the endpoint. Defaults to application/json if not specified.

description.description:
Human-readable description of the endpoint. Think of this as metadata describing
the endpoint

http_timeout.description: Define endpoint http timeout in seconds.

is_disabled.description: This is used to manually enable/disable the endpoint.

mtls_client_cert.description: mTLS client certificate configuration for the endpoint

owner_id.description:
The OwnerID is used to group more than one endpoint together to achieve
[fanout](https://getconvoy.io/docs/manual/endpoints#Endpoint%20Owner%20ID)

rate_limit.description:
Rate limit is the total number of requests to be sent to an endpoint in
the time duration specified in RateLimitDuration

rate_limit_duration.description: Rate limit duration specifies the time range for the rate limit.

secret.description: Endpoint's webhook secret. If not provided, Convoy autogenerates one for the endpoint.

slack_webhook_url.description:
Slack webhook URL is an alternative method to support email where endpoint developers
can receive failure notifications on a slack channel.

support_email.description:
Endpoint developers support email. This is used for communicating endpoint state
changes. You should always turn this on when disabling endpoints are enabled.

url.description:
URL is the endpoint's URL prefixed with https. non-https urls are currently
not supported.

app_id.description: Deprecated but necessary for backward compatibility.

custom_headers.description: Specifies custom headers you want convoy to add when the event is dispatched to your endpoint

data.description:
Data is an arbitrary JSON value that gets sent as the body of the
webhook to the endpoints

endpoint_id.description: Specifies the endpoint to send this event to.

event_type.description: Event Type is used for filtering and debugging e.g invoice.paid

idempotency_key.description: Specify a key for event deduplication

category.description: Category is a product-specific grouping for the event type

description.description: Description is used to describe what the event type does

json_schema.description: JSONSchema is the JSON structure of the event type

name.description: Name is the event type name. E.g., invoice.created

event_type.description: Type of event this filter applies to (required)

body_function.description:
Function is a javascript function used to mutate the payload
immediately after ingesting an event

custom_response.description:
Custom response is used to define a custom response for incoming
webhooks project sources only.

header_function.description:
Function is a javascript function used to mutate the headers
immediately after ingesting an event

idempotency_keys.description:
IdempotencyKeys are used to specify parts of a webhook request to uniquely
identify the event in an incoming webhooks project.

provider.description: Use this to specify one of our predefined source types.

pub_sub.description:
PubSub are used to specify message broker sources for outgoing
webhooks projects.

verifier.description:
Verifiers are used to verify webhook events ingested in incoming
webhooks projects. If set, type is required and match the verifier
type object you choose.

app_id.description: Deprecated but necessary for backward compatibility

function.description:
Convoy supports mutating your request payload using a js function. Use this field
to specify a `transform` function for this purpose. See this[https://docs.getconvoy.io/product-manual/subscriptions#functions] for more

custom_headers.description: Specifies custom headers you want convoy to add when the event is dispatched to your endpoint

data.description:
Data is an arbitrary JSON value that gets sent as the body of the
webhook to the endpoints

event_type.description: Event Type is used for filtering and debugging e.g invoice.paid

event_types.description: A list of event types for the subscription filter config

idempotency_key.description: Specify a key for event deduplication

secret.description: Endpoint's webhook secret. If not provided, Convoy autogenerates one for the endpoint.

url.description:
URL is the endpoint's URL prefixed with https. non-https urls are currently
not supported.

data.description:
Data is an arbitrary JSON value that gets sent as the body of the
webhook to the endpoints

expiration.description:
Amount of time to wait before expiring the old endpoint secret.
If AdvancedSignatures is turned on for the project, signatures for both secrets will be generated up until
the old signature is expired.

custom_headers.description: Specifies custom headers you want convoy to add when the event is dispatched to your endpoint

data.description:
Data is an arbitrary JSON value that gets sent as the body of the
webhook to the endpoints

event_type.description: Event Type is used for filtering and debugging e.g invoice.paid

idempotency_key.description: Specify a key for event deduplication

owner_id.description: Used for fanout, sends this event to all endpoints with this OwnerID.

event_types.description: List of event types that the subscription should match

ids.description: A list of event delivery IDs to forcefully resend.

client_cert.description: ClientCert is the client certificate PEM string

client_key.description: ClientKey is the client private key PEM string

expiry_time_unit.description: Expiry time unit (seconds, milliseconds, minutes, hours)

field_mapping.description: Field mapping for flexible token response parsing

dp.description: RSA first factor CRT exponent (RSA private key only)

dq.description: RSA second factor CRT exponent (RSA private key only)

p.description: RSA first prime factor (RSA private key only)

q.description: RSA second prime factor (RSA private key only)

qi.description: RSA first CRT coefficient (RSA private key only)

duration.description: Used to specify a valid Go time duration e.g 10s, 1h3m for how long to wait between event delivery retries

interval_seconds.description: Used to specify a time in seconds for how long to wait between event delivery retries,

retry_count.description: Used to specify the max number of retries

payload.description: Sample payload to test against the filter (required)

is_match.description: Whether the payload matches the filter criteria

advanced_signatures.description:
Convoy supports two [signature formats](https://getconvoy.io/docs/product-manual/signatures)
-- simple or advanced. If left unspecified, we default to false.

authentication.description:
This is used to define any custom authentication required by the endpoint. This
shouldn't be needed often because webhook endpoints usually should be exposed to
the internet.

content_type.description: Content type for the endpoint. Defaults to application/json if not specified.

description.description:
Human-readable description of the endpoint. Think of this as metadata describing
the endpoint

http_timeout.description: Define endpoint http timeout in seconds.

is_disabled.description: This is used to manually enable/disable the endpoint.

mtls_client_cert.description: mTLS client certificate configuration for the endpoint

owner_id.description:
The OwnerID is used to group more than one endpoint together to achieve
[fanout](https://getconvoy.io/docs/manual/endpoints#Endpoint%20Owner%20ID)

rate_limit.description:
Rate limit is the total number of requests to be sent to an endpoint in
the time duration specified in RateLimitDuration

rate_limit_duration.description: Rate limit duration specifies the time range for the rate limit.

secret.description: Endpoint's webhook secret. If not provided, Convoy autogenerates one for the endpoint.

slack_webhook_url.description:
Slack webhook URL is an alternative method to support email where endpoint developers
can receive failure notifications on a slack channel.

support_email.description:
Endpoint developers support email. This is used for communicating endpoint state
changes. You should always turn this on when disabling endpoints are enabled.

url.description:
URL is the endpoint's URL prefixed with https. non-https urls are currently
not supported.

category.description: Category is a product-specific grouping for the event type

description.description: Description is used to describe what the event type does

json_schema.description: JSONSchema is the JSON structure of the event type

event_type.description: Type of event this filter applies to (optional)

is_flattened.description: Whether the filter uses flattened JSON paths (optional)

body_function.description:
Function is a javascript function used to mutate the payload
immediately after ingesting an event

custom_response.description:
Custom response is used to define a custom response for incoming
webhooks project sources only.

forward_headers.description: Soecfy header you want convoy to save from the ingest request and forward to your endpoints when the event is dispatched.

header_function.description:
Function is a javascript function used to mutate the headers
immediately after ingesting an event

idempotency_keys.description:
IdempotencyKeys are used to specify parts of a webhook request to uniquely
identify the event in an incoming webhooks project.

is_disabled.description: This is used to manually enable/disable the source.

pub_sub.description:
PubSub are used to specify message broker sources for outgoing
webhooks projects, you only need to specify this when the source type is `pub_sub`.

verifier.description:
Verifiers are used to verify webhook events ingested in incoming
webhooks projects.  If set, type is required and match the verifier
type object you choose.

app_id.description: Deprecated but necessary for backward compatibility

function.description:
Convoy supports mutating your request payload using a js function. Use this field
to specify a `transform` function for this purpose. See this[https://docs.getconvoy.io/product-manual/subscriptions#functions] for more
